steps.title: Factor the common term
steps.content: Both terms share a greatest common factor of $2xy$. Factor that common term out of the expression, then find what remains inside the parentheses.

steps.title: Divide and match
steps.content:
Divide each original term by $2xy$.
$$\frac{16x^3y^2}{2xy}=8x^2y$$
$$\frac{14xy}{2xy}=7$$
So the factored expression is $2xy(8x^2y+7)$. A calculator table can check that the original expression and this factored expression match for test values.
$$x_1=[2,3]$$
$$y_1=[3,4]$$
$$o_1=16x_1^3y_1^2+14x_1y_1$$
$$b_1=2x_1y_1(8x_1^2y_1+7)$$
The answer is B.
